To seek the truth we must

); ``` ## Props | Prop | Type | Default | Description | | ------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------- | -------- | ----------------------------------------------------- | | `text` | `string \| string[]` | - | Text to type. Pass an array to cycle through phrases. | | `as` | `ElementType` | `"div"` | Element or component to render as. | | `speed` | `number` | `50` | Typing speed in ms per character. | | `initialDelay` | `number` | `0` | Delay before typing starts in ms. | | `waitTime` | `number` | `2000` | Pause before deleting or moving to next phrase in ms. | | `deleteSpeed` | `number` | `30` | Deleting speed in ms per character. | | `loop` | `boolean` | `true` | Whether to loop through the phrases. | | `className` | `string` | - | Classes applied to the wrapper element. | | `showCursor` | `boolean` | `true` | Whether to show the cursor. | | `hideCursorOnType` | `boolean` | `false` | Hide the cursor while typing/deleting. | | `cursorChar` | `string \| React.ReactNode` | `"\|"` | Character or node rendered as the cursor. | | `cursorClassName` | `string` | `"ml-1"` | Classes applied to the cursor element. | | `cursorAnimationVariants` | `{ initial: Variants["initial"]; animate: Variants["animate"] }` | blinking | Animation variants for the cursor. |

title: Split Action
description: Interactive component for split actions.
---

import { SplitActionDemo } from "@/registry/new-york/split-action-demo";

<ComponentPreview name="split-action">
  <SplitActionDemo />
</ComponentPreview>

## Installation

If you've already added the prsui registry, use the short command:

<CodeBlockCommand
  __bun__="bunx --bun shadcn@latest add @prsui/split-action"
  __npm__="npx shadcn@latest add @prsui/split-action"
  __pnpm__="pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @prsui/split-action"
  __yarn__="yarn shadcn@latest add @prsui/split-action"
/>

Otherwise, use the full registry URL:

<CodeBlockCommand
  __bun__="bunx --bun shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/split-action.json"
  __npm__="npx shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/split-action.json"
  __pnpm__="pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/split-action.json"
  __yarn__="yarn shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/split-action.json"
/>

## Usage

After you add the component with the CLI, import it from your UI folder:

```tsx
import { SplitActions } from "@/components/ui/split-action";

export function MyComponent() {
  const actions = [
    { icon: PenTool, label: "Edit" },
    { icon: Share2, label: "Share" },
  ];
  return (
    <SplitActions
      actions={actions}
      onActionClick={(action, index) => {
        console.log(`Clicked ${action.label} at index ${index}`);
      }}
    />
  );
}
```

## Props

| Prop            | Type                                      | Default | Description                                                                                                     |
| --------------- | ----------------------------------------- | ------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `actions`       | `Action[]`                                | -       | Array of action objects with `icon` and `label`                                                                 |
| `triggerIcon`   | `LucideIcon`                              | `Plus`  | Icon for the trigger button                                                                                     |
| `onActionClick` | `(action: Action, index: number) => void` | -       | Callback fired when an action button is clicked. The component closes automatically after an action is clicked. |

### Action

| Property | Type         | Description                       |
| -------- | ------------ | --------------------------------- |
| `icon`   | `LucideIcon` | Icon displayed in the button      |
| `label`  | `string`     | Text label displayed next to icon |

--- title: Mouse Follow description: Animated cursor follow effects with spring physics using framer-motion. --- import { MouseFollowDemo } from "@/registry/new-york/mouse-follow-demo"; ## Installation If you've already added the prsui registry, use the short command: Otherwise, use the full registry URL: ## Usage After you add the component with the CLI, import it from your UI folder: ```tsx "use client"; import { SpringMouseFollow, SimpleMouseFollow } from "@/components/ui/mouse-follow"; export function MyComponent() { return (
); } ``` ## Components ### SimpleMouseFollow A basic cursor follower that tracks pointer position with no spring physics — instant and direct. ### SpringMouseFollow A cursor follower with spring physics for smooth, bouncy motion. Includes opacity and scale transitions on enter/leave. ## Spring Config | Property | Type | Default | Description | | --------- | -------- | ------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------- | | `mass` | `number` | `0.1` | How sluggish or responsive the object feels. Lower = snappier. | | `damping` | `number` | `10` | Resistance to oscillation. Higher = less bounce. | | `stiffness` | `number` | `131` | Spring tension. Higher = faster return to origin. |
